questions.question: Which HTML element is commonly used as the foundation for a navigation menu?
questions.explanation: An unordered list (<ul>) with list items (<li>) containing links is the standard, semantic way to build a navigation menu.

questions.question: Which CSS property removes the bullet points from a list?
questions.explanation: Setting list-style: none on a <ul> or <ol> removes the default bullet points or numbers from list items.

questions.question: How can you make list items appear side by side in a horizontal menu?
choices: Set list items to display: block
Set list items to display: inline or use flexbox
questions.explanation: Setting display: inline or display: inline-block on list items, or using flexbox on the parent <ul>, arranges menu items horizontally.

questions.question: Which CSS property removes the default underline from links?
questions.explanation: The text-decoration: none property removes the default underline that browsers apply to anchor (<a>) tags.

questions.question: The <___> tag wraps around navigation links to indicate a navigation section.
questions.explanation: The <nav> tag is a semantic HTML5 element that identifies a section of navigation links on the page.

questions.question: Each item in a list is wrapped in an <___> tag.
questions.explanation: The <li> (list item) tag defines each individual item inside an ordered (<ol>) or unordered (<ul>) list.
